title: 1. Crafting system instructions and behavioral constraints

blocks.content: System instructions set the identity, style, and parameters of the agent's behavior. They are injected at the root layer of model calls and cannot be cleared by conversation updates.

blocks.code:
settings:
  model: "gemini-1.5-pro"
  system_instructions: |
    You are a professional, compliance-focused credit analysis agent.
    Your responses must be structured, objective, and contain no speculation.
    You are forbidden from stating interest rates directly unless quoting from the lookup tool.

blocks.title: Root Enforcement
blocks.content: Unlike user prompts, model architectures prioritize system instructions, making them the primary layer to configure safety and style bounds.

Question 1: Where are system instructions registered in an ADK application?
- A) Inside local environment files
- B) Under settings.system_instructions in agent.yaml
- C) Inside the tool Python decorator code

Feedback: Option B is correct. The settings block defines the agent's system prompt instructions.
